etymology.description: Interpreted as inverted 之 (footprint) or roots (柢)
etymology.interpretation: The character 帀 (zā, 'to surround') is uncertain in its origins. It is traditionally interpreted in Shuowen as an inverted version of 之 (zhī, 'footprint'), originally 㞢, symbolizing encirclement, while another interpretation compares it to roots (柢, 'roots'), suggesting a surrounding network.
